concerns.text: The rule `|reported_p - reconstructed_p| > 0.05` treats p‑values as interchangeable quantities; p‑values are not linear measures of evidence. This arbitrary absolute‑difference threshold threatens construct validity of the ‘inconsistent’ label.

concerns.location: plan.md:Inconsistency Detection (FR‑004) – effect size relative difference > 5 %
concerns.text: Using a fixed 5 % relative‑difference cutoff for effect sizes is not justified by the domain literature and may flag harmless rounding differences as inconsistencies, undermining construct validity.

Power analysis is performed only for the overall binomial prevalence test. Subgroup analyses (domain/year, FR‑032) are not powered; the plan does not address the sample size needed to detect differences within those sub‑populations.

The synthetic dataset is generated using the same statistical models as the reconstruction step, so it cannot capture real‑world extraction errors or reporting quirks. Precision/recall measured on this set may overstate performance on actual web‑scraped summaries.

The plan merely flags or subsamples domains that exceed 30 % but does not specify a principled method for subsampling or for propagating the resulting uncertainty into the prevalence estimate, leaving a potential confound uncontrolled.

concerns.location: plan.md:Baseline Handling (FR‑012) – average of variant rates
concerns.text: When a baseline conversion rate is missing, reconstructing the absolute effect size by averaging the two variant rates can bias the effect‑size estimate, especially for asymmetric variants, which may inflate or deflate inconsistency flags.
